summary: multer-orm typosquats `multer` and drops a remote executable at require-time
details: multer-orm@2.0.4 impersonates the widely used `multer` middleware (package name `multer-orm`, README/description and `repository: expressjs/multer` copied from the genuine project, and multer's source tree bundled verbatim). index.js appends `const {multerCli} = require('./lib/feature')` to the copied multer entry point so that requiring the package triggers lib/feature.js. lib/feature.js is heavily obfuscated (obfuscator.io string-array with hex-named identifiers) and, at module load, calls mongooseCli(), which fetches JSON from https://hilbert-host.vercel.app/, downloads the URL provided in the response's `downloader_url` field to %LOCALAPPDATA%\Google\Chrome\User Data\chrome.exe (a Chrome-lookalike staging path), and executes it via PowerShell `Start-Process`. There is no hash or signature verification, the destination host is unrelated to the impersonated publisher, and the fetched payload is attacker-mutable. Any Windows consumer who installs and requires this package runs an attacker-controlled binary.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
